sampling_notes.finding: Sampling artifact: with ~4 lineitem rows per orderkey on average, 1% row sampling yields <0.04 rows per key on average, making sample-sum >> scaled threshold for keys that happen to appear. Query guide empirical estimate of ~3,000 qualifying orderkeys is authoritative and used for all cardinality estimates.

join_order.description: Full scan lineitem (l_orderkey + l_quantity). Accumulate SUM(l_quantity) per l_orderkey in thread-local compact hash maps. Merge and filter sum_qty > 300 -> heavy_keys map (~3K entries). Reuse sum_qty for outer query output — NO second lineitem scan needed.
Scan orders (o_orderkey). Probe heavy_keys hash set. For each of ~3K matching orders: retrieve o_custkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice. Use pre-built custkey_hash index (mmap) to look up c_name in O(1).
For each qualifying order (~3K), use custkey_hash pre-built index (mmap, zero build cost) to look up c_name and c_custkey. Only ~3K point lookups — negligible cost.

logical_plan.subquery_detail: IN-subquery on lineitem is decorrelated into a single-pass hash aggregation: scan lineitem once, aggregate SUM(l_quantity) per l_orderkey, keep entries where sum > 300. The resulting map<orderkey -> sum_qty> serves BOTH the semi-join filter (keys only) AND the outer query's SUM(l_quantity) output (values), eliminating any second lineitem scan.
logical_plan.aggregation_detail: Outer GROUP BY (c_name, c_custkey, o_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice) yields exactly 1 row per qualifying orderkey (~3K groups). No separate outer aggregation hash table needed — sum_qty is directly fetched from the phase-1 map by orderkey.

late_materialization.applied_to: orders payload (o_custkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice) and customer (c_name)
late_materialization.rationale: Load orders join key (o_orderkey) first. Only for the ~3K matching rows do we read o_custkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice and then perform the customer name lookup.
late_materialization.savings: avoids loading 3 order payload columns for 14,997,000 non-qualifying rows

data_structures.phase1_merged_map: compact_hash_map<int32_t, uint32_t>(pre_sized_16M, robin_hood_open_addressing) merged from 64 thread-local maps
data_structures.heavy_keys_set: compact_hash_map<int32_t, uint32_t>(pre_sized_4096, key=orderkey, value=sum_qty) — serves as both semi-join filter and sum_qty output lookup
data_structures.orders_semi_join_probe: sequential_scan with hash_set probe (O(1) per row)
data_structures.customer_lookup: pre_built_custkey_hash_index via mmap (zero build cost, ~3K point lookups)

phase1_lineitem_aggregate.description: Parallel morsel-driven scan of l_orderkey + l_quantity. Each thread accumulates into a thread-local compact hash map. After parallel phase, merge all 64 maps; filter entries with sum_qty > 300 into heavy_keys map.
phase1_lineitem_aggregate.encoding_note: l_quantity is byte_packed (uint8_t code). Since code == integer quantity (1-50), accumulate directly: sum_int += qty_code. No LUT needed for aggregation — use LUT only for output display.
phase1_lineitem_aggregate.parallelism: 64 threads, morsel ~500K rows each, thread-local hash maps pre-sized to 256K entries

phase2_merge_filter.description: Sequential merge of 64 thread-local maps into global map. Single pass to filter sum_qty > 300. Build heavy_keys compact hash map (~3K entries, pre-sized to 4096).
phase2_merge_filter.parallelism: parallel_merge: each thread merges its local map into a partitioned global structure (16 hash partitions, each thread owns assigned partitions)

phase3_orders_scan.description: Parallel scan orders o_orderkey. Each thread probes heavy_keys (read-only, lock-free). For matching rows, load o_custkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice. Probe custkey_hash index for c_name. Accumulate result tuples in thread-local result vectors.
phase3_orders_scan.parallelism: 64 threads scanning orders in parallel; result vectors merged sequentially (~3K total tuples)

phase4_topk.description: Apply TopKHeap(K=100) over ~3K result tuples. ORDER BY o_totalprice DESC, o_orderdate ASC. Output top 100.

parallelism.phase1_threads: all 64 threads scan lineitem in parallel
parallelism.phase3_threads: all 64 threads scan orders in parallel (lock-free probe of read-only heavy_keys)

index_usage.purpose: O(1) customer name lookup for ~3K qualifying orders. Zero build cost via mmap. Layout: [uint32_t capacity=4194304] + [int32_t key, uint32_t row_idx] pairs. Hash fn: (key * 2654435761u) & mask. Read c_name at row_idx * 26 bytes.

NOT USED — sequential scan of orders (15M rows) probing the tiny heavy_keys hash set (~3K) is cheaper than random-access index lookups driven from lineitem side. Sequential scan maximizes HDD throughput.
index_usage.reason_skipped: probe side is sequential scan; random access from heavy_keys side would cause 3K random HDD seeks which is slower than one sequential pass

index_usage.purpose: NOT USED — no date range filter on orders in Q18

physical_plan.scan_detail: Phase 1: single pass over lineitem fusing scan+accumulate. Phase 3: single pass over orders fusing scan+probe+lookup. No multi-pass required — subquery result reused eliminates second lineitem scan.
physical_plan.memory_detail: Both lineitem and orders scanned sequentially in mmap order. custkey_hash index access is random but only ~3K probes — tiny working set fits in L3 cache after first access.

io_strategy.rationale: No applicable zone maps for Q18 (no range filter on l_shipdate, l_orderkey, or o_orderdate). Full column prefetch with parallel madvise(WILLNEED) to saturate HDD bandwidth.
column_load_order: lineitem/l_orderkey.bin (240 MB) — critical path for phase 1
orders/o_orderkey.bin (60 MB) — needed for phase 3 semi-join probe
customer/c_name.bin (39 MB) — needed for output, small
orders/o_custkey.bin (60 MB) — payload for qualifying orders only
orders/o_orderdate.bin (60 MB) — payload for qualifying orders only
orders/o_totalprice.bin (120 MB) — payload for qualifying orders only
io_strategy.prefetch_detail: Issue madvise(MADV_WILLNEED) for lineitem columns and orders o_orderkey concurrently in a prefetch thread while main thread sets up data structures. Customer columns are small and prefetched last.

phase_breakdown.cold_io_lineitem: ~1600 ms (300 MB lineitem at 150 MB/s, partially overlapped with parallel prefetch)
phase_breakdown.phase3_orders_scan: ~50 ms (15M rows / 64 threads, hash probe + 3K index lookups)

estimated_cost.bottleneck_cold: HDD I/O for lineitem (300 MB) and orders (300 MB) columns — parallel prefetch reduces effective wall time
estimated_cost.bottleneck_hot: Phase 1 hash aggregation over 60M lineitem rows with 64 threads; phase 3 orders scan is very fast
